post.description: Kick off a new blueprint installation job. Returns 202 Accepted with Location header pointing to the job resource

source_blueprint_file.description: URL to the blueprint zip file

source_auth_token.description: Auth token with access to the source org. Required for cross-org auto-verification when the caller's bearer token belongs to the destination org.

destination_auth_token.description: Auth token with access to the destination org. Defaults to the caller's bearer token.

slug.description: Slug to enforce in this blueprint. Used in marketplace blueprints to keep it consistent with webflow.

auto_enable_features.description: If true, automatically enable required features in the destination org before installing

get.description: Get Blueprint by ID

post.description:
Start a blueprint validation job. Validates Terraform for the blueprint (all types).
Returns 202 Accepted with job_id. Poll GET /jobs/{job_id} for status, valid, and errors.

Start a blueprint verification job. Compares resource configurations between a source org
and a destination org to verify that a sync/install was successful.
Returns 202 Accepted with job_id. Poll GET /jobs/{job_id} for status, summary, and resource_results.

source_org_id.description: Organization ID of the source org

destination_org_id.description: Organization ID of the destination org

source_auth_token.description: Auth token with access to the source org (e.g. pipeline token). If not provided, the caller's bearer token is used for both orgs.

destination_auth_token.description: Auth token with access to the destination org. Required for cross-org verification when the caller token only has access to the source org. If not provided, the caller's bearer token is used for both orgs.

installation_job_id.description: Optional install job this verification is checking. If omitted, the latest destination blueprint installation job is used when available.

sync_engine.description: Optional install engine hint. Usually inferred from installation_job_id.

post.description:
Detect changes between the current state of a blueprint's resources and its tfstate baseline.
Returns field-level diffs for resources that have been modified since the blueprint was last installed/exported.

source_org_id.description: Organization ID of the source org where changes were made

dest_org_id.description: Organization ID of a destination org (used to load tfstate baseline)

dest_blueprint_id.description: Blueprint ID in the destination org (used to locate tfstate in S3)

post.description: Apply a patch to a single destination org.

destination_auth_token.description: Auth token with access to the destination org (e.g. pipeline token)

post.description: Retry a failed patch execution for a specific org.
Kick off a new blueprint export job. Returns 202 Accepted with Location header pointing to the job resource.

validate.description: When true, run terraform validate before creating the export zip. If validation fails, the job will be marked as FAILED with errors.

get.description:
List all available marketplace blueprint slugs from Webflow CMS.
Returns cached results when available.

slug.description: The installation slug identifier for the blueprint (used for publishing).

marketplace_slug.description: The marketplace page slug for the Webflow CMS item (used for display).

post.description: Publish a blueprint to the marketplace. Exports the blueprint, uploads it to file-api with public access, and updates the Webflow CMS listing.

slug.description: The marketplace installation slug for the Webflow CMS item

version.description: The version string to set on the marketplace CMS item (e.g. "1.0.0")

name.description: The display name for the blueprint on the marketplace CMS item

post.description: Format a blueprint description as markdown using AI.

text.description: Plain text to format as markdown

post.description:
Suggest resources to add to a blueprint based on a natural-language prompt.

Walks anchor resource types in priority order (journey > workflow_definition >
automation_flow > schema > entity-backed types) and returns matches per
anchor using each upstream API's text search. Suggestions are marked
`is_root: true` so callers can pass `add_dependencies=true` to
bulkAddBlueprintResources and have transitive dependencies resolved
server-side — which means a single matched journey can stand in for its
full product/schema/template bundle.

No side effects on the blueprint — the caller persists the chosen resources
via the existing create/bulk-add endpoints.
Add a resource to a Blueprint

schema.description: Whether to add this resource dependencies to the blueprint automatically

post.description: Sync dependencies of all root resources in a Blueprint

parameters.name: trigger
parameters.description:
What initiated the sync. Automated triggers (`pre_sync`,
`post_revert`) are side effects of an operation the activity
feed already shows on its own row, so they are excluded from
the audit log — same policy as validate/verify.

post.description: Bulk Add resources in a Blueprint

schema.description: Whether to add this resource dependencies to the blueprint automatically

put.description: Bulk update resources in a Blueprint

delete.description: Bulk delete resources in a Blueprint

delete.description: Delete a resource from a Blueprint

get.description: List all blueprint jobs

Poll the current state of a job. Serves both Terraform (v2) and V3-engine jobs —
check `sync_engine` (`terraform` | `v3`) to tell them apart. V3 jobs additionally
expose live `resource_progress[]`. V3 single-install and bulk-install child jobs
are polled here.

post.description:
Resume an installation job that is paused at `status: "WAITING_USER_ACTION"` after
planning. Works for both Terraform and V3 jobs. Not needed for V3 installs created
with `auto_apply: true` (including all bulk-install child jobs), which apply
without pausing.
Cancel a blueprint job if it is still running.
Create a marketplace listing for a blueprint. Returns 409 if one already exists.

409.description: Listing already exists for this blueprint

get.description: Get marketplace listing for a blueprint including all versions
List all marketplace listings for the authenticated organization
Get marketplace listing by listing ID including all versions

post.description: Create a draft version; auto-snapshots resources, requiredFeatures, recommendedApps from current blueprint

get.description: List all versions for a listing, newest first

patch.description: Update updateNote, requiredFeatures, or recommendedApps on a draft version

post.description: Publish a draft version; archives the previous live version

400.description: Version not publishable or version_name already taken

post.description: Starts an asynchronous V3 publication. The result is a signed, portable package; poll the existing blueprint job endpoint for completion.

Install a blueprint into a single destination org using the V3 engine (direct API
calls, no Terraform). Creates resources in topological order with global ID
replacement and supports checkpoint-based resume on failure.

**Lifecycle (how to drive an install to completion):**
1. `POST /v3/blueprint-manifest/blueprint:install` returns `{ job_id }` (202).
2. Poll the job with `GET /v2/blueprint-manifest/jobs/{job_id}` — V3 jobs are
   served by the same v2 jobs endpoints as Terraform jobs and are identified by
   `sync_engine: "v3"`. Watch `status` and `resource_progress[]`.
3. If `auto_apply` is `false` (default), the job pauses at
   `status: "WAITING_USER_ACTION"` after planning. Resume it with
   `POST /v2/blueprint-manifest/jobs/{job_id}:continue`.
4. If `auto_apply` is `true`, the engine applies automatically after plan +
   snapshot — no `:continue` call is needed. This is what the bulk-install worker
   uses; to install into many orgs at once prefer `POST .../bulk-installs`.

For cross-org installs, pass `destination_auth_token` (the destination org's
token); reads use the caller's bearer token, writes use that token.

source_blueprint_file.description: S3 key to the blueprint zip file

destination_auth_token.description: Auth token for the destination org. Required for cross-org installs where the caller's token belongs to the source org. Defaults to the caller's bearer token.

auto_apply.description:
When `true`, the install skips the manual plan-approval step and applies
straight after plan + snapshot succeed (no `:continue` call needed).
Defaults to `false`. Used internally by the bulk-install worker.

Location.description: Path to poll the resulting job: GET /v2/blueprint-manifest/jobs/{job_id}

400.description: Missing required source or destination fields

post.summary: Restore a specific deployment by job_id
post.description:
Roll a deployment back to its pre-install state. Two phases:

  1. Upsert — re-applies the captured payloads via snapshot-api's
     `:restore` (server-side; runs config-engine.apply with captured
     target ids pre-seeded). Skipped for pure-create deployments
     whose snapshot was empty.
  2. Delete sweep — for lineage rows of this blueprint instance not
     present in the snapshot's captured set, deletes the live
     resource via the type's adapter. Co-ownership / drift /
     no-delete-capability skip the entry with the corresponding
     reason.

Resolves `(blueprint_id, job_id)` to the entry in
`Blueprint.deployments[]` and reads its `snapshot_id` and
`destination_blueprint_id` — the caller never needs to handle
snapshot ids directly.

Async — returns 202 with a job id. Poll the job to track progress.
The per-instance lock (`installation_status === 'IN_PROGRESS'`)
rejects concurrent installs or restores with 409.

400.description: Deployment has no snapshot, or the snapshot is not in a `completed` state.

404.description: Blueprint not found, or no deployment with the given job_id on this blueprint.

409.description: Another install or restore is in flight for this blueprint instance.

get.summary: Predicted outcome of reverting a deployment
get.description:
Computes what would happen if the user triggered a restore on this
deployment, without performing any writes. The forecast uses the
snapshot's captured resources (when present) plus the current lineage
state plus per-adapter gates (co-ownership, no-delete-capability,
heuristic-match, drift when wired).

Idempotent and side-effect free. Safe to call repeatedly. The result
may shift between calls if operators edit destination resources or
another blueprint adopts a shared resource in the meantime.

parameters.name: job_id
parameters.description: The install job whose deployment is being previewed.

200.description:
Predicted outcome. `failed`-action items are absent here — the
preview can't forecast errors.

get.description:
Returns the lineage registry entries for a blueprint's resources in the current org.
Shows the mapping between source lineage IDs and target resource IDs.

post.description:
Install one source blueprint into many destination organizations in a single
request. The server schedules child V3 installs with `auto_apply=true` and caps
active installs at `max_concurrency`. Per-target failures are isolated and
retryable; they do not stop the remaining targets.

Each target carries its own write-only `destination_auth_token` (org-scoped).
Tokens are passed to the worker via Step Functions input only — they are never
persisted in DynamoDB nor returned by any endpoint.

twoTargets.summary: Install one blueprint into two orgs

Location.description: URL to poll the bulk install

400.description:
Validation error — missing `source_blueprint_id`, empty/oversized `targets`
(1–100), duplicate `destination_org_id`, missing per-target
`destination_auth_token`, or `max_concurrency` out of range (1–5).

get.description:
Returns the bulk install parent with aggregate status and counts. Scoped by the
caller org as `source_org_id`. Target rows are not included — use the targets
endpoint to page through them.

404.description: Bulk install not found for the caller org

get.description:
Pages through the bulk install's target rows. Each row hydrates its latest child
install job (`job_ids.at(-1)`) with the standard V3 job shape (`events[]`,
`resource_progress[]`) so callers can inspect per-resource progress and errors.

404.description: Bulk install not found for the caller org

post.description:
Retries a single failed target. Allowed only for `FAILED` and `PARTIAL_SUCCESS`
targets. Starts a new child install with `auto_apply=true`, appends its job id to
`job_ids`, and reuses the same target row. Only the destination auth token may be
supplied for the new attempt; source/destination identifiers are immutable.

destination_auth_token.description: Write-only auth token for the destination org used for the new attempt.

200.description: Updated target row with hydrated latest child job

400.description: Target is not in a retryable (`FAILED`/`PARTIAL_SUCCESS`) state.

404.description: Bulk install or target not found for the caller org.

409.description: The target was concurrently claimed by another retry (already in progress).

get.description:
List all custom uniqueness criteria configured for the caller's organization.
These overrides are applied during install (V2 and V3) when matching incoming
resources against existing ones in the destination org, replacing the default
per-resource-type field set with the caller's chosen fields (AND-combined).

200.description: All configured criteria for the org

defaults.description:
Built-in default uniqueness fields per resource type, as used by
the install engines when no custom criteria are configured.

readonly_types.description:
Resource types whose criteria are fixed by epilot and cannot be
customized (writes are rejected). Shown read-only in the UI.

get.description: Get the configured uniqueness criteria for a specific resource type, if any.

200.description: Configured criteria for the resource type

404.description: No custom criteria configured (defaults will be used)

put.description:
Set or replace the uniqueness criteria for a resource type. The provided fields
must be valid attributes on the resource's schema (the UI typically loads the
schema to populate options). All listed fields are AND-combined during matching.

propagated_to.description:
Org IDs this rule was also applied to (the UI's "Also apply to"
selection). Stored so the selection survives reloads; each target
org still holds its own criteria row.

400.description: Invalid resource type or field list

delete.description: Remove the custom criteria for a resource type, reverting to the default fields.

UniquenessCriteriaResourceType.description: Resource type for which custom uniqueness criteria can be configured.

target_id.description: Resource ID in the destination org

preinstall.description: Markdown content shown before starting to install the blueprint
preinstall.example: This is the content of the preinstall.md file which contains the blueprint description.

postinstall.description: Markdown content to be displayed when showing the plan to install blueprint
postinstall.example: This is the content of the postinstall.md file

job_id.description: Blueprint installation job that created or updated this deployment record

restore_details.description: Restore lifecycle metadata for this deployment.

has_revertible_changes.description:
Whether this sync changed destination resources in a way that can
be reverted. `false` means the sync completed without create,
update, internal-update, or delete impacts, so there is no revert
action to offer.

resource_impact_summary.description: Counts of resource impact values from the V3 apply result.

last_restore_job_id.description:
BlueprintInstallationJob id of the most recent restore that ran
against this deployment. Used by the FE to keep the restore-status
badge visible across page reloads. Frontends poll this job to
render the latest restore outcome.

last_restore_at.description:
Timestamp of the most recent restore that ran against this
deployment. Stamped when the restore sweep finishes. Used by the
FE to show when a sync was reverted.

last_restored_by.description:
Identity of the caller who triggered the most recent restore.
Stamped when the restore sweep finishes. Used by the FE to show
who reverted a sync.

name.description: Display name (email or token name) of the restorer.

user_id.description: User id of the restorer, when triggered by a user.

is_verified.description: Whether the blueprint is verified by epilot

ignored_resource_addresses.description: Resource addresses excluded during the latest install/update and ignored by verification.

source_blueprint_id.description: ID of the blueprint that brought this blueprint to the destination org (deployed or installed)

archived.description: Whether the blueprint is archived (soft-deleted). Archived blueprints are hidden from the main list.

recommended_apps.description: List of recommended app IDs for the blueprint

enabled.description: Features that must be enabled in the target org

disabled.description: Features that must be disabled in the target org

zip_file_name.description: Custom name for the exported zip file

prompt.description: Natural-language description of what to include.
prompt.example: everything for the hausanschluss use case

blueprint_id.description:
When provided, suggestions are scoped as additions to this existing
blueprint — resources already in it are excluded from the response.

resources.description: Suggested resources to add. All marked is_root so the caller can request transitive dependency resolution.

suggested_blueprint_name.description:
Short title derived from the prompt. Useful when the caller is
creating a new blueprint as a result of the suggestion — saves the
user from naming it themselves.

explanation.description: Short human-readable summary of what was matched and why.

add_dependencies_recommended.description:
Hint to the caller: persist via bulkAddBlueprintResources with
?add_dependencies=true so anchor resources (journeys, workflows)
pull their transitive dependencies.

is_root.description: When a resource is marked as root, we'll be able to keep track of it's dependencies

is_ready.description: when editing a blueprint, this indicates if the resource is ready to be exported and when installing a blueprint, this indicates if the resource is ready to be used

is_hidden.description: When a resource is marked as hidden, it's used to hide it from the UI

is_disabled.description: When a resource is marked as disabled, it will be skipped during export

parent_resource_ids.description: Used to automatically remove resources with hard dependencies and to block deletion of resources with hard dependencies

depends_on_addresses.description: Terraform addresses this resource references (for dependency-aware ignore)

impact_on_install_reason.description: Fields causing the updates / internal updates on a resource install

BlueprintPreview.description: Preview data for a blueprint before installation. Stored temporarily with TTL.

blueprint_file_s3_key.description: S3 key of the blueprint zip file

is_updating.description: Whether the blueprint is updating to the latest version in the marketplace

recommended_apps.description: List of recommended app IDs for the blueprint

has_update_available.description: Whether a newer version is available in the marketplace.

latest_marketplace_version.description: The latest version available in the marketplace.

installation_link.description: URL to install/update the blueprint from the marketplace.

InstalledMarketplaceBlueprintItem.description: Summary of an installed marketplace blueprint for version tracking

slug.description: URL slug of the blueprint from the epilot marketplace

has_update_available.description: Whether a newer version is available in the marketplace

latest_marketplace_version.description: The latest version available in the marketplace

installation_link.description: URL to install/update the blueprint from the marketplace

sync_engine.description: Engine used for this install job

resource_progress.description: Per-resource live status. Populated only for V3 installs.

BulkInstallStatus.description:
Aggregate status for a bulk install or one of its targets.
- `QUEUED`: not started yet
- `IN_PROGRESS`: at least one target queued/in-progress, not all done
- `SUCCESS`: all targets succeeded
- `PARTIAL_SUCCESS`: all targets terminal with a mix of success/partial/failure
- `FAILED`: all targets terminal and none succeeded or partially succeeded

BulkInstallCounts.description: Tally of target rows by status. Recomputed from target rows on each transition.

BulkInstall.description: Bulk install parent. Never carries target auth tokens.

BulkInstallTarget.description:
A single destination of a bulk install. `job` is the hydrated latest child
install job derived from `job_ids.at(-1)`. Auth tokens are never stored or returned.

job.description: The hydrated latest child install job (`job_ids.at(-1)`), when present.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. Absent on the last page.

destination_auth_token.description: Write-only org-scoped token for the destination org. Never persisted or returned.

source_org_id.description:
The org that owns the source blueprint. Optional; defaults to the caller org and,
if provided, must equal it — source reads use the caller's token, and the bulk job
is owned/polled by the caller org. A different value is rejected with 400.

max_concurrency.description: Maximum number of concurrently active child installs.

install_job_id.description:
The install job whose deployment is being reverted. Maps back
to the entry in `Blueprint.deployments[]`.

snapshot_id.description:
The snapshot driving Phase 1 of the restore. Null for sweep-only
restores (pure-create deployments with no captured manifest).

restore_result.description: Absent while the job is still IN_PROGRESS.

action.description:
On `restore-preview`: the action the restore would take.
On `restore_result`: the action that was applied.
`failed` only appears on `restore_result`.

last_synced_at.description: Only set when `reason == modified`. From the lineage row's last install write.

current_updated_at.description: Only set when `reason == modified`. From the destination resource's current state.

is_hidden.description:
Mirrors the install manifest's `is_hidden` for this resource —
helper resources (entity mappings, datasources, flow-template
automations) the UI hides on every other resources view. Absent
on rows from legacy installs without a persisted manifest.

co_owned_by.description:
Only set when `reason == co_owned`. The other live blueprint
instances that still own this resource's lineage row — the
"another sync" the skip refers to.

snapshot_id.description:
The snapshot driving Phase 1 of the restore. Null/absent for
sweep-only restores (pure-create deployments with no captured
manifest).

has_effective_changes.description:
`true` iff at least one entry in `resources` has an effective
action (`action` is `restore` or `delete`). `false` when every
resource would be skipped (all `skip` / `failed`). Consumers
can gate the "Revert sync" confirm button on this — if false,
executing the revert is a no-op.

valid.description: Present when status is SUCCESS or FAILED.

installation_job_id.description: Install job this verification is checking, when known.

sync_engine.description: Install engine used by the linked installation job, when known.

resource_results_s3_key.description: S3 key for detailed results when too large for inline storage.

failure_context.description: Explains whether this result is likely downstream of another failed resource.

path.description: JSON path to the differing field (e.g. "steps[2].name")

resources_to_ignore.description: List of resource addresses to ignore changes for. When a resource is marked as create, it will be ignored and not created.

ManifestID.description: ID of an imported / installed manifest

JobID.description: ID of an import or export job (state machine)

previous_jobs_ids.description: List of job IDs that were used to install the manifest

previous_jobs.description: List of jobs that were used to install the manifest

changes_reason.description: Fields causing the updates / internal updates on a resource install

source_id.description: Original ID of the exported resource

changes_reason.description: Fields causing the updates / internal updates on a resource install

manifest_file_path.description: The S3 key of the manifest file

plan_file_content.description: An URL to download the plan file

resources_to_export.description: An array of tree-like JSON objects or a singular tree-like JSON object representing the resources to import

large_resources_to_export_url.description: An URL to download the resources to export when the resources are too large to be included in the response

resources_to_import.description: An array of tree-like JSON objects or a singular tree-like JSON object representing the resources to import

large_resources_to_import_url.description: An URL to download the resources to import when the resources are too large to be included in the response

is_verified.description: Whether the manifest comes from a trusted source and is signed by epilot

source_blueprint_slug.description: URL slug of a blueprint from the epilot marketplace

source_blueprint_file.description: A URL to download the source blueprint file used to import the blueprint

install_status_description.example: This blueprint installation resulted in a partial deployment; some resources were created successfully, but  failed to complete the full resource setup.

is_verified.description: Whether the manifest comes from a trusted source and is signed by epilot

selected_resources_url.description: All the resources that were selected to be exported, used to pre-select the resources when updating a sandbox manifest

ready_imported_resources_url.description: All the resources that were marked as ready by the user

source_organization_id.description: ID of the organization that deployed the manifest

source_manifest_id.description: ID of the manifest that was deployed

last_triggered_at.description: When the deployment was last triggered

deployed_from.description: Information about the manifest that was deployed from, used to update existing deployments instead of always creating new ones

destination_organization_id.description: ID of the organization that the manifest was deployed to

destination_manifest_id.description: ID of the manifest that was deployed to

last_triggered_at.description: When the deployment was last triggered

deployed_to.description: Information about the manifest that was deployed to, used to update existing deployments instead of always creating new ones

created_at.description: When the manifest was first installed (applied)

updated_at.description: When the manifest was last updated (applied)

imported_resources.description: An array of tree-like JSON objects or a singular tree-like JSON object representing the resources to import

large_imported_resources_url.description: An URL to download the imported resources when the resources are too large to be included in the response

markdown.description: Markdown content part of a manifest file

manifest.description: Markdown content shown before installing the manifest
manifest.example: This is the content of the manifest.md file which contains the manifest descripton.

preinstall.description: Markdown content to be displayed when showing the plan to install blueprint
preinstall.example: This is the content of the preinstall.md file

postinstall.description: Markdown content to be displayed when showing the plan to install blueprint
postinstall.example: This is the content of the postinstall.md file

PreInstallRequirements.description: List of feature settings that must be enabled before installing the blueprint

name.description: a human readable name of the caller (e.g. user name, token name or email address)

user_id.description: epilot user id, when called by a user

token_id.description: token id, when called by API token

pipeline_id.description: Pipeline ID selected when doing the sandbox sync

originalAddress.description: Original resource ID to be replaced

replacementId.description: ID of the resource that will replace the original

replacementName.description: Name of the resource that will replace the original

ready_resources.description: Array of ids of resources selected to be ready by the user

destination_organization_id.description: ID of the organization that the manifest was deployed to

destination_manifest_id.description: ID of the manifest that was deployed to

JobID.name: job_id
JobID.description: ID of an import or export job

EpilotAuth.description: Authorization header with epilot OAuth2 bearer token
